title: Stripe and the Whispering Bamboo
description: A prehistoric tiger cub discovers his true self beneath the rustling bamboo grove, learning that his stripes are not just marks but the map of his journey.
text:
[SINGING] Beneath the golden, setting sun,
The bamboo sways—a whispered tune,
Stripe the cub with amber eyes,
Wonders why the wind still sighs.

[GENTLE] Are my stripes too bold, too bright?
Do they hide me in the night?
Or are they threads of stories old,
Woven deep in earth’s embrace so cold?

[HUMMING] Hmm-hmm-hmm, the leaves hum low,
Hmm-hmm-hmm, the river knows.

[JOYFUL] Oh, little cub, don’t fear the light,
Your stripes are stars that guide the night!
They dance with fireflies in flight,
And paint the sky with dreams so bright!

[PAUSE]

[SINGING] One evening, as the crickets play,
Stripe met Owl on moonlit spray.
"Why do you hide?" the wise one said,
"Your stripes are roots where courage’s fed."

[GENTLE] The river hums a lullaby,
Of ancient tales beneath the sky.
Of cubs who learned to stand so tall,
Not despite their stripes—but all.

[HUMMING] Hmm-hmm-hmm, the earth sings deep,
Hmm-hmm-hmm, the secrets keep.

[JOYFUL] Oh, little cub, don’t fear the dark,
Your stripes are embers, glowing spark!
They light the path where dreams embark,
And lead you home through meadows’ arc!

[PAUSE]

[SLEEPY] Now Stripe curls ‘neath bamboo’s grace,
His stripes no longer just a trace.
But maps of love, of time, of place,
A cub who found his truest face.

[GENTLE] So close your eyes, let dreams take flight,
The bamboo sings you through the night.
For every stripe, for every hue,
Is proof that you are brave—and true.

[HUMMING] Hmm-hmm-hmm, the stars align,
Hmm-hmm-hmm, the world is kind.

morals: Your differences are not flaws—they are the unique threads that weave your strength and story.
True belonging begins when you embrace the beauty of who you are, without apology.
